questions.question: What does `grid-template-rows: auto 1fr auto` create when combined with `min-height: 100vh`?
options: A full-page layout with sized header/footer and flexible main content
questions.explanation: `auto 1fr auto` creates a header (auto → content-sized), a main area (1fr → fills remaining space), and a footer (auto → content-sized). With `min-height: 100vh`, the grid fills the viewport.

questions.question: Why might `grid-template-rows: 20% 60% 20%` NOT work as expected?
options: The container may not have an explicit height
Percentages are not supported in rows
The fr unit is required for rows
CSS Grid does not support row percentages
questions.explanation: Row percentages require the container to have an explicit height. Without one, percentage values default to `auto`, and the rows size to their content.

questions.question: How many rows does `grid-template-rows: repeat(3, 100px)` create?
questions.explanation: `repeat(3, 100px)` creates 3 explicit rows, each exactly 100px tall.

questions.question: What does `grid-row: 2 / 4` do?
options: Spans from row line 2 to row line 4 (covers 2 rows)
questions.explanation: `grid-row: 2 / 4` places the item starting at line 2 and ending at line 4, spanning rows 2 and 3 (2 rows in total).

questions.question: What is the purpose of `grid-auto-rows`?
options: Controls the height of implicitly created rows
Creates rows based on viewport height
questions.explanation: `grid-auto-rows` sets the height for rows that are created automatically when items exceed the explicit row definitions.

questions.question: In a grid with `grid-template-rows: repeat(2, 100px); grid-auto-rows: 80px` and 10 items in a 3-column layout, how tall is row 3?
questions.explanation: Rows 1 and 2 are explicit (100px). Row 3 is an implicit row, so it uses `grid-auto-rows: 80px`.

questions.question: What happens if a grid container has `grid-template-rows: 1fr` but no height or min-height set?
options: The row collapses because fr has no space to distribute
questions.explanation: `1fr` needs space to distribute. Without a container height or min-height, there's no reference point, so the row collapses to 0 or auto.

questions.question: What does `grid-row: span 3` do?
options: Makes the item span across 3 rows
questions.explanation: `grid-row: span 3` makes the item stretch across 3 row tracks from its starting position.

questions.question: Which is the correct syntax for named row lines?
questions.explanation: Named grid lines use square brackets: `[line-name] size [next-line-name]`. They sit between track sizes.

questions.question: What is the `grid-row` shorthand a combination of?
questions.explanation: `grid-row` is shorthand for `grid-row-start` and `grid-row-end`, separated by a forward slash: `grid-row: 1 / 3`.

questions.question: How does `grid-template-rows: 1fr 1fr` differ from `grid-template-rows: auto auto`?
options: 1fr fills available space equally; auto sizes to content
1fr is for columns only; auto is for rows only
questions.explanation: `1fr` rows distribute available vertical space evenly. `auto` rows size to the height of their content, which may be smaller or larger.

questions.question: What is the minimum number of row lines for a grid with 3 explicit rows?
questions.explanation: N rows create N+1 lines. Three rows have lines 1, 2, 3, and 4.

questions.question: Which of these creates a row that is at least 200px but can grow?
questions.explanation: `minmax(200px, auto)` sets a minimum height of 200px and allows the row to grow (auto) as needed for content.

questions.question: Can `grid-auto-rows` accept multiple values to create repeating implicit row patterns?
options: No, it only accepts a single value
questions.explanation: `grid-auto-rows` can accept multiple values that repeat in a cycle. `grid-auto-rows: 80px 120px` alternates between 80px and 120px for implicit rows.

questions.question: What happens if a grid item is placed at row line 5 but only 3 explicit rows exist?
options: Implicit rows are created to accommodate the placement
The item is placed at the last available row
The grid breaks and shows nothing
questions.explanation: CSS Grid creates implicit rows to accommodate items placed beyond the explicit grid. The implicit row sizes come from `grid-auto-rows`.

questions.question: What does `grid-row: 1 / -1` do?
options: Spans from the first row line to the last row line
questions.explanation: `grid-row: 1 / -1` makes the item span from the first line (1) to the last line (-1), covering all rows.

questions.question: Why is `min-height: 100vh` commonly paired with `grid-template-rows: auto 1fr auto`?
options: To ensure the grid fills at least the viewport height
questions.explanation: `min-height: 100vh` ensures the grid container is at least as tall as the viewport, giving the `1fr` row space to fill. If content is short, the footer still sticks to the bottom.

questions.question: Which property would you use to make all implicitly-created rows have the same height?
questions.explanation: `grid-auto-rows` controls the height of all implicit rows. Setting it to a single value (e.g., `grid-auto-rows: 120px`) makes all implicit rows the same height.

questions.question: In a grid spanning full viewport, what is the effect of `grid-template-rows: 80px 1fr 80px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 300px;`?
options: Creates a layout with 80px header/footer, flexible main content, and a 300px sidebar
The layout has 6 grid cells total (2 columns × 3 rows)
questions.explanation: This creates 3 rows (80px, 1fr, 80px) × 2 columns (1fr, 300px) = 6 cells. Header/footer span across by default, with a main content area and a 300px sidebar.

questions.question: What's the key difference between explicit and implicit rows?
options: Explicit rows are defined by `grid-template-rows`; implicit rows are created automatically
Explicit rows are visible; implicit rows are hidden
Explicit rows use fr; implicit rows use px
questions.explanation: Explicit rows are those you explicitly define with `grid-template-rows`. Implicit rows are created by the browser when grid items are placed beyond the explicit grid tracks.
